Or a portion of that list or whatever. So the number one thing to look for in an email service provider is deliverability. In other words, your ability to actually get into the inbox of the people you send emails to. Now, for those who don't know this world of online marketing yet, they say, well, that's no big deal. So the ability to put 1000 people on your list of 500 people for free. They have rotten deliverability as low as like 70 to 80%, which means that 20 to 30% of all the emails you're sending out are not even arriving. Not just in the inbox, but even arriving at all for the people you're sending it to. That's huge. If you've got 10,000 people on your list, that means that two to 3000 of them don't even get your in your emails. Not that they go to spam or something else. They aren't there. If you ask them to search for it, they won't find it because it never actually arrived. And so I strongly encourage you not to use anybody that has a free option when it comes to email service providers. The next concept is called tagging versus lists.
